source_file.description: Repo-relative path of the authoring document. No leading slash; no parent-directory segments — the field is display-only today but is constrained here so a future consumer that reads the file cannot be tricked into traversing outside the repo.

pattern.description: Match-engine input. For 'forbidden_text' / 'required_text' the value is a Python regex (re.search). For 'filename_glob_forbidden' the value is a globstar pattern: '**' crosses path separators, '*' matches within one segment, '?' matches one non-slash character. A pattern with no '/' is treated as unanchored — '*.env' fires on 'configs/.env' as well as on a root-level '.env'. Patterns containing at least one '/' are interpreted strictly so authors keep an escape hatch when they need path-anchored matching.

allOf.$comment: filename_glob_forbidden makes sense only against the diff scope. Encode the runtime constraint here so a schema-valid file cannot trip a runtime BLOCK.
